rulings.Case Title: STEVE RYAN VS. MITCHELL ROSENFELD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Vacate Judgmnt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 17 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF STEVE RYAN Motion To Vacate Judgment. The matter is continued to March 17, 2015 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than February 17, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL A. TRUFANT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Arrest Judgment Of 5-23-14 Pursuant To Order Continuing Case Management Conference Of 8-25-14
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 17 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CAROL TRUFANT, PHD Motion To Arrest Judgment Of 5-23-14 Pursuant To Order Continuing Case Management Conference Of 8-25-14. Denied. Judgment was entered in favor of Defendant on November 25, 2014. This motion does not establish a basis to set aside that judgment. To the extent that Plaintiff is seeking reconsideration of the Court's order granting judgment on the pleadings, such a motion is time-barred. (See CCP sec. 1008.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BITDEFENDER SRL, A ROMANIAN LIMITED LIABILITY VS. 5381 PARTNERS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 17, 2015, Line 2, Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions Off calendar. Duplicate entry.

rulings.Case Title: BITDEFENDER SRL, A ROMANIAN LIMITED LIABILITY VS. 5381 PARTNERS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 17, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF BITDEFENDER SRL, A ROMANIAN LIMITED LIABILITY's Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for issue sanctions based on defendant's failure to comply with the discovery Order of this court dated December 2, 2014 is granted. The defendant did not file opposition to the motion. A hearing is required. The moving party shall prepare a proposed Order detailing the precise facts or issues to be covered by the issue sanction Order. The request for monetary sanctions is granted as against defendant Steve Grossman in the sum of $1,500.00 to be paid within twenty days of the date of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: SHOPOFF CAVALLO & KIRSH LLP VS. PETER PAPPAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 17, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF SHOPOFF CAVALLO & KIRSH LLP's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Cross-defendant's motion to compel further responses to the request for production of documents is granted. Cross-complainant's objections are overruled. Service of the discovery request just prior to expiration of the parties' voluntary stay of discovery was reasonable under the circumstances. Cross-complainants production of boxes of documents at the deposition was in response to and required pursuant to the deposition notice and that proceeding. Cross-defendant's decision to not copy same or question the witnesses regarding the documents has no relationship to and does not alter the obligation to respond to a separate document production notice. Cross-complainants shall serve their verified further response and simultaneously produce all non-privileged documents within fifteen days of the date of this Order. To the extent any document is claimed to be privileged, cross- complainants shall identify same in a privilege log setting forth the date of the writing, writer and recipient, and basis for the specific privilege asserted. The Court assumes there will be substantial amount of documents to which privilege is asserted. The parties shall meet and confer prior to the hearing in an attempt to agree on a Discovery Referee to rule on privilege issues. If the parties cannot agree, each side shall propose a Discovery Referee and the Court shall choose. Cross-defendant's request for sanctions is granted pursuant to CCP 2031.310(h) as against cross-complainant Peter Pappas and his counsel in the sum of $3,500.00, to be paid within 15 day of the date of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel plaintiff to provide a verified answer interrogatories and verified response to the document request is granted. Plaintiff hall have thirty days from the date of this Order to accomplish same. Based on the facts and circumstances set forth in the opposition, the request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling.
